title: PLINDER: The protein-ligand interactions dataset and evaluation resource
task: protein-ligand interaction dataset curation; leakage-controlled train/test split construction; protein-ligand docking generalization evaluation
datasets: PLINDER; PLINDER-NR; PLINDER-PL50; PLINDER-TIME; PLINDER-ECOD; PoseBusters; PDBBind; DockGen
models_or_methods: PLINDER; leakage-minimizing split generation; protein/pocket/interaction/ligand-level similarity annotation; paired apo and predicted structure annotation
metrics: protein/pocket/interaction/ligand similarity metrics; leakage / train-test similarity; test set quality; ligand RMSD; top-1 docking success rate
review_notes: Revised to keep PLINDER and its splits as the main dataset objects. DiffDock retraining is an evaluation protocol/baseline use, not a proposed model method in this paper.

title: EquiBind: Geometric Deep Learning for Drug Binding Structure Prediction
task: drug-protein binding structure prediction; blind protein-ligand docking; receptor binding-location prediction; ligand bound-pose and orientation prediction; ligand torsion refinement
datasets: PDBBind v2020; PDBBind time-based split; PDBBind 2019 test set
models_or_methods: EquiBind; SE(3)-equivariant geometric deep learning; direct-shot binding pose prediction; keypoint-based rigid alignment; von Mises torsion-angle conformer fitting
metrics: ligand RMSD / L-RMSD; centroid distance; Kabsch-RMSD; percentage below 2 Å; percentage below 5 Å; RMSD percentiles; runtime / inference speed
review_notes: Added Kabsch-RMSD because it is explicitly listed in the evaluation metrics. QuickVina/SMINA are fine-tuning/baseline components, not core proposed method terms.

title: DiffDock: Diffusion Steps, Twists, and Turns for Molecular Docking
task: molecular docking; protein-ligand binding pose prediction; blind docking; generative modeling of ligand poses; confidence estimation for docking poses
datasets: PDBBind; PDBBind time split / 2019 test complexes; computationally folded protein structure evaluation setting
models_or_methods: DiffDock; diffusion generative model over ligand poses; diffusion on translational, rotational and torsional degrees of freedom; non-Euclidean manifold diffusion; confidence model for pose ranking
metrics: top-1 RMSD < 2 Å success rate; ligand RMSD; centroid distance; percentage below 2 Å; percentage below 5 Å; RMSD percentiles; selective accuracy; inference time
review_notes: Centroid distance and top-1/top-5 threshold metrics are explicitly reported in appendix metrics. Computationally folded structures are an evaluation setting, not a separate named dataset.

title: SE(3) Stochastic Flow Matching for Protein Backbone Generation / FoldFlow
task: protein backbone generation; de novo protein structure generation; unconditional protein backbone design; generative modeling over SE(3); equilibrium conformation generation
datasets: PDB subset / PDB-derived 22,248-protein training set; BPTI molecular-dynamics trajectory evaluation set
models_or_methods: FoldFlow; FoldFlow-Base; FoldFlow-OT; FoldFlow-SFM; SE(3) stochastic flow matching; Riemannian optimal transport; simulation-free continuous-time dynamics; stochastic continuous-time dynamics over SE(3)
metrics: designability; scRMSD; diversity / average pairwise TM-score; novelty / max TM-score to PDB or training set; fraction with max TM-score < 0.5 and scRMSD < 2 Å; iterations per second; KL divergence for conformation sampling
review_notes: Removed vague “protein backbones up to 300 aa” from datasets. Kept it as task/evaluation scope rather than a dataset name.

title: FrameFlow: Fast Protein Backbone Generation with SE(3) Flow Matching
task: protein backbone generation; de novo protein design; fast protein backbone sampling; SE(3)-frame generation
models_or_methods: FrameFlow; SE(3) flow matching; FrameDiff adaptation to flow matching; FramePred architecture; Invariant Point Attention; self-conditioning; SE(3) inference scheduler
metrics: designability; scRMSD; diversity / structural clusters; novelty / pdbTM; TM-score; sampling timesteps; sampling speedup
review_notes: FrameDiff, Genie and RFdiffusion are baselines and should not be listed as proposed methods.

title: DiffBindFR: An SE(3) Equivariant Network for Flexible Protein-Ligand Docking
paper_type: non-flow flexible docking baseline / model paper
task: flexible protein-ligand docking; full-atom protein-ligand binding-structure prediction; ligand binding-pose prediction; protein pocket side-chain conformation prediction; apo and AlphaFold2-structure-based docking
datasets: PDBbind v2020; PDBbind time split; PoseBusters test set; Apo dataset; AlphaFold2 modeled structures
models_or_methods: DiffBindFR; full-atom diffusion-based flexible docking; SE(3)-equivariant network; product-space diffusion over ligand rotation/translation/torsion and pocket side-chain torsions; MDN scoring model
metrics: ligand RMSD; centroid distance; RMSD < 2 Å; RMSD < 5 Å; pocket side-chain RMSD; PoseBusters validity; steric clashes / physical plausibility; runtime
review_notes: Removed generic “cross-docking benchmark” unless it is explicitly used as a named evaluation set. Interaction accuracy should be manually checked if later added.

title: Improved Motif-Scaffolding with SE(3) Flow Matching
paper_type: flow matching conditional protein design model
task: motif scaffolding; conditional protein backbone generation; functional motif-conditioned protein design; scaffold diversity improvement
datasets: benchmark of 24 biologically meaningful motifs; PDB monomer training set
models_or_methods: FrameFlow for motif scaffolding; SE(3) flow matching; motif amortization; motif guidance; conditional score estimation; motif data augmentation
metrics: designability / solved motifs; number of unique designable motif-scaffolds; scaffold diversity; motif RMSD; self-consistency RMSD / scRMSD; TM-score-based clustering; sampling speed
review_notes: Kept “benchmark of 24 motifs” because the abstract explicitly states it. Removed vague “motif-scaffolding benchmark” duplication.

title: Deep Confident Steps to New Pockets: Strategies for Docking Generalization / DockGen
task: blind docking generalization assessment; protein binding-pocket benchmark construction; docking to unseen protein classes; confidence bootstrapping for diffusion docking
datasets: DockGen; PDBBind; Binding MOAD; DockGen benchmark; PDBBind benchmark
models_or_methods: DockGen benchmark construction; Confidence Bootstrapping; DiffDock scaling/retraining strategy; synthetic data strategy
metrics: RMSD < 2 Å success rate; ligand RMSD; binding-site similarity; BLOSUM62 similarity; harmonic mean similarity; train-test leakage / contamination; generalization performance
review_notes: DockGen is both a benchmark object and appears in the title; keep it as dataset/benchmark, not as a proposed model method. DiffDock is the base model, not the main proposed method.

title: FlowMol: Mixed Continuous and Categorical Flow Matching for 3D De Novo Molecule Generation
task: 3D de novo molecule generation; joint generation of atom positions and atom/bond categorical variables; unconditional molecular generation
models_or_methods: FlowMol; SimplexFlow; mixed continuous and categorical flow matching; probability-simplex categorical flow; GVP-based molecular generator; flow matching with explicit hydrogens and formal charges
metrics: percent atoms stable; percent molecules stable; percent molecules valid; Jensen-Shannon divergence of potential energy distributions; inference time
review_notes: Removed “training loss” because it is not an evaluation metric under the current schema. Kept only metrics explicitly described in model evaluation.

title: FoldFlow-2: Sequence-Augmented SE(3)-Flow Matching for Conditional Protein Backbone Generation
paper_type: flow matching conditional protein generation model
task: sequence-conditioned protein backbone generation; conditional protein structure generation; unconditional protein backbone generation; equilibrium conformation sampling; motif/scaffold conditional protein design
datasets: PDB; high-quality synthetic protein structures; large-scale PDB+synthetic structure training set; VHH nanobody scaffold design set
models_or_methods: FoldFlow-2; sequence-augmented SE(3)-flow matching; protein language model conditioning; multi-modal fusion trunk; geometric transformer decoder; Reinforced Finetuning / ReFT
metrics: designability; diversity; novelty; scRMSD; TM-score; LDDT; secondary-structure diversity; reward alignment metrics; equilibrium conformation sampling performance
review_notes: Dataset names are partly descriptive rather than canonical. Keep them conservative and verify exact names before final gold.
verification_status: mostly verified; dataset names need final manual check

title: SemlaFlow: Efficient 3D Molecular Generation with Latent Attention and Equivariant Flow Matching
task: unconditional 3D molecular generation; joint molecular graph and 3D conformation generation; efficient molecular sampling
models_or_methods: Semla; SemlaFlow; scalable E(3)-equivariant message passing; latent attention; equivariant flow matching; joint generation of atom types, coordinates, bond types and formal charges
metrics: atom stability; molecule stability; validity; uniqueness; novelty; connectedness; number of function evaluations / NFE; sampling speed; distributional benchmark metrics
review_notes: Kept molecular generation metrics only. Do not add docking/RMSD metrics for this paper.

title: PoseBench: Benchmarking the Robustness of Pose Estimation Models under Corruptions
task: human and animal pose estimation robustness evaluation; anatomical keypoint detection under image corruptions
datasets: PoseBench corruption benchmark; human pose estimation datasets; animal pose estimation datasets
models_or_methods: PoseBench; corruption benchmark construction; top-down pose estimation evaluation; bottom-up pose estimation evaluation; heatmap-based/regression-based/classification-based pose-estimation comparison
metrics: pose estimation accuracy; robustness under corruption; keypoint detection performance; degradation under blur/noise/compression/lighting/mask corruptions
review_notes: Exclude from molecular/protein-ligand benchmark. This is not protein-ligand PoseBusters/PoseBench; it is image-based human/animal keypoint pose estimation.

title: AtomFlow: Design of Ligand-Binding Proteins with Atomic Flow Matching
paper_type: flow matching ligand-binding protein design model
task: ligand-binding protein design; protein backbone generation conditioned on a target molecule; ligand conformation generation; joint ligand-protein structure generation from a 2D molecular graph
datasets: PDBBind; SCOPe; RFDiffusionAA selected ligand evaluation set (FAD, SAM, IAI, OQO); extended ligand set
models_or_methods: AtomFlow; atomic flow matching; unified biotoken representation; SE(3)-equivariant structure prediction network; iterative ligand conformation and protein backbone generation
metrics: self-consistency; binding affinity; diversity; novelty; scRMSD; Vina score / binding score; ligand RMSD; design success rate
review_notes: Replaced vague “PDB subset” with explicit PDBBind and SCOPe. Evaluation set from RFDiffusionAA should be kept as an evaluation collection, not a standard dataset name.

title: PocketFlow: Generalized Protein Pocket Generation with Prior-Informed Flow Matching
paper_type: flow matching protein pocket generation model
task: protein pocket generation; ligand-binding protein pocket design; generation of high-affinity and valid pockets; generalized pocket design across small molecules, peptides and RNA
datasets: CrossDocked; Binding MOAD; small-molecule / peptide / RNA ligand benchmark sets
models_or_methods: PocketFlow; prior-informed flow matching; protein-ligand interaction priors; hydrogen-bond interaction modeling; binding affinity guidance; interaction geometry guidance; multi-granularity guidance
metrics: AAR / amino acid recovery; scRMSD; Vina Score; hydrogen-bond recovery; interaction recovery; binding affinity; pocket validity; ligand-modality generalization performance
review_notes: Removed PDBBind because the main experimental datasets shown for small-molecule pocket design are CrossDocked and Binding MOAD. Keep PDBBind only if later evidence shows a main evaluation table uses it.

title: FLOWR: Flow Matching for Structure-Aware De Novo, Interaction- and Fragment-Based Ligand Generation
paper_type: flow matching pocket-conditioned ligand generation model
task: structure-aware de novo ligand generation; protein-pocket-conditioned ligand generation; interaction-conditioned ligand generation; fragment-based ligand generation; ligand optimization
datasets: SPINDR; CrossDocked2020; ligand-pocket co-crystal complexes; protein-pocket ligand-generation benchmarks
models_or_methods: FLOWR; FLOWR.MULTI; continuous and categorical flow matching; equivariant optimal transport; protein pocket conditioning; interaction-profile conditioning; fragment/substructure conditioning
metrics: PoseBusters validity; pose accuracy; interaction recovery; inference speedup; Vina score; diversity; novelty; uniqueness; chemical validity; fragment and interaction constraint satisfaction
review_notes: SPINDR is explicitly introduced as a curated dataset. PoseBusters-validity is a metric, not a dataset.

title: PoseBusters: AI-based docking methods fail to generate physically valid poses or generalise to novel sequences
task: physical validity evaluation for protein-ligand docking; docking-method benchmarking; generalization assessment to novel sequences; chemical and geometric consistency checking
datasets: PoseBusters benchmark set; PDBBind / protein-ligand docking benchmark collections
models_or_methods: PoseBusters; PoseBusters test suite; RDKit-based chemical validity checks; post-prediction energy minimization evaluation protocol; molecular-mechanics force-field minimization
metrics: RMSD to native binding mode; PB-valid / PoseBusters validity; ligand stereochemistry correctness; aromatic ring planarity; bond length validity; bond angle validity; intramolecular validity; intermolecular steric clash checks; physical plausibility; generalization performance
review_notes: DiffDock, EquiBind, TankBind, Uni-Mol, AutoDock Vina and CCDC Gold are compared methods, not proposed methods.

title: TANKBind: Trigonometry-Aware Neural Networks for Drug-Protein Binding Structure Prediction
task: drug-protein binding structure prediction; protein-ligand complex conformation prediction; blind docking; binding-site prediction; protein-ligand affinity prediction
datasets: PDBBind; PDBBind 2019 time-split test set; binding-structure and affinity benchmark datasets
models_or_methods: TANKBind; trigonometry-aware neural network; protein functional block segmentation; pairwise distance-map prediction; contrastive losses with local-region negative sampling; joint binding interaction and affinity optimization
metrics: ligand RMSD; centroid distance; RMSD percentiles; percentage below 2 Å; percentage below 5 Å; affinity prediction correlations; runtime; number of parameters
review_notes: Affinity metrics should be checked from affinity tables before final gold if exact names such as Pearson/Spearman/RMSE are needed.
verification_status: verified; affinity metric names need final check

title: DynamicBind: Predicting Ligand-Specific Protein-Ligand Complex Structure with a Deep Equivariant Generative Model
task: ligand-specific protein-ligand complex structure prediction; flexible docking from unbound protein structures; protein conformational change prediction; cryptic pocket identification; virtual screening
datasets: PDBbind; Major Drug Target / MDT test set; antibiotics benchmark; Cryptosite dataset / cryptic-pocket case studies
models_or_methods: DynamicBind; equivariant geometric diffusion network; dynamic docking; ligand-specific conformational transition modeling; smooth energy landscape construction; cLDDT confidence/ranking module; binding-affinity prediction module
metrics: ligand RMSD; fraction of ligand RMSD < 2 Å; fraction of ligand RMSD < 5 Å; clash score; stringent success criterion (ligand RMSD < 2 Å and clash score < 0.35); relaxed success criterion (ligand RMSD < 5 Å and clash score < 0.5); pocket RMSD; cLDDT; auROC for cLDDT selection; virtual-screening auROC
review_notes: Removed unsupported DockQ-like/interface-quality wording. The source describes cLDDT, pocket RMSD, ligand RMSD thresholds, clash score and AUROC.

title: Accurate Structure Prediction of Biomolecular Interactions with AlphaFold 3
task: biomolecular complex structure prediction; protein-ligand interaction prediction; protein-nucleic acid interaction prediction; protein-protein and antibody-antigen interaction prediction; prediction of complexes with ions and modified residues
datasets: PoseBusters v1; recent PDB evaluation set / RecentPDBEval; CASP15 RNA targets; PDB; CCD-based ligand and modification evaluation sets
models_or_methods: AlphaFold 3; diffusion-based structure prediction model; unified biomolecular interaction model; Pairformer; diffusion module; confidence model; cross-distillation strategy
metrics: pocket-aligned ligand RMSD < 2 Å; interface LDDT; LDDT; DockQ > 0.23; GDT; confidence-ranked top sample accuracy; covalent modification success rate; RNA target accuracy
review_notes: AF3 is a model/method, not a dataset. The paper explicitly reports metrics by interaction type, including ligand RMSD, interface LDDT, LDDT and DockQ threshold.
